description: Today every command requires the full 'bl-xxxx' id (bl show bl-534c, bl close bl-534c, bl claim bl-534c). The 'bl-' is pure noise — there is exactly one id namespace and the prefix never disambiguates anything. Accept the bare hex form everywhere a task id is taken: bl show 534c, bl close 534c, bl claim 534c, bl update 534c --note ..., bl dep add 534c 1e60, etc. Implementation: normalize once at the CLI boundary (probably a small helper that prepends 'bl-' if the arg matches /^[0-9a-f]+$/ and isn't already prefixed), so the storage layer keeps using full ids and serialized task files are unchanged. Keep accepting the prefixed form for backward compatibility and for copy-paste from delivery tags. Tests: every command that takes an id gets a bare-hex case alongside its existing prefixed case.
title: Accept task ids without bl- prefix on the CLI
